GraceRequirements.Annotation: Curated, opinionated OpenCode process layer for spec-first, review-driven, safer agentic development with managed agents, managed skills, semantic model roles, workflow tracking, safety plugins, and the vvoc CLI.
GraceRequirements.Keywords: opencode, plugins, workflow, model-roles, hashline-edit, work-items, controller, managed-agents, managed-skills, bun, typescript

Users.User: Engineer who wants a repeatable OpenCode workflow with spec-first planning, review gates, safer edits, portable model choices, and long-run safety.
Team or maintainer who wants the same agent workflow to be reproducible across machines, repositories, and model providers.
Repository maintainer who needs deterministic CLI, plugin, package, config, release, and verification behavior.

Goals.Goal: Provide a curated process layer on top of OpenCode so agentic development follows explicit trajectories instead of ad-hoc prompt discipline.
Support spec-first and plan-first work through managed skills that produce durable XML artifacts before large implementation waves.
Make implementation review-driven by separating focused implementation, spec review, code review, verification, and bounded retry loops.
Keep shared agents and skills portable by using semantic model roles such as vv-role:smart and resolving them per machine or project.
Make multi-agent work explicit through work items, tracked subagent headers, reviewer result collection, hard stops, and bounded review rounds.
Improve long-run safety with Guardian permission review, secrets redaction, hashline-backed edits, strict config validation, and clear recovery diagnostics.
Provide one CLI, vvoc, for setup, sync, launch, status, diagnostics, roles, presets, plugin toggles, completions, and upgrades.

UC-001.Name: SetupAndInstall
UC-001.Action: User runs vvoc init or vvoc install for global or project scope.
UC-001.Goal: Bootstrap vv-opencode into OpenCode with managed package registration, config, agents, skills, default controller selection, and role-reference model fields.
UC-001.Acceptance: The selected scope contains the pinned package registration, canonical vvoc config, seeded built-in roles and presets, managed agents and skills, vv-controller default agent registration, and vv-role:* model references.

UC-002.Name: ManagedWorkflowSync
UC-002.Action: User runs vvoc sync after package updates, config drift, or moving to another machine.
UC-002.Goal: Refresh vvoc-owned config, prompts, skills, presets, plugin registration, and managed OpenCode agent registrations without clobbering user-owned state.
UC-002.Acceptance: Valid current vvoc config is rewritten canonically while role assignments, user presets, plugin toggles, and unrelated OpenCode entries are preserved; invalid existing config fails before mutation with recovery guidance.

UC-003.Name: DeterministicLaunch
UC-003.Action: User runs vvoc launch with effective, project, or global scope.
UC-003.Goal: Start OpenCode with deterministic OPENCODE_CONFIG and VVOC_CONFIG selection for the requested scope.
UC-003.Acceptance: Launch selects the expected config paths, forwards passthrough arguments, preserves child exit code, and reports missing project scope setup with a clear install hint.

UC-004.Name: StatusAndDiagnostics
UC-004.Action: User runs vvoc status, vvoc doctor, or related diagnostics.
UC-004.Goal: Explain current installation, selected config sources, role assignments, plugin registration, and recoverable setup problems without mutating config.
UC-004.Acceptance: Diagnostics report selected source kind and path, validation failures, unresolved role problems, plugin state, and next-action guidance while leaving files unchanged.

UC-005.Name: SemanticModelRoles
UC-005.Action: Shared OpenCode agents, subagents, commands, or root model fields use vv-role:<role-id> references.
UC-005.Goal: Let shared workflow definitions express model intent semantically while each machine or project resolves roles to concrete provider/model selections.
UC-005.Acceptance: Supported model-bearing fields resolve at startup from the selected vvoc config, valid variants are represented safely, literal non-role values are left alone, and unknown or invalid roles fail loudly.

UC-006.Name: RoleManagement
UC-006.Action: User runs vvoc role list, set, or unset.
UC-006.Goal: Inspect and manage built-in and custom semantic role assignments in the selected vvoc config.
UC-006.Acceptance: Built-in roles are visible, custom lowercase-hyphenated roles can be added or removed, concrete assignments normalize consistently, and built-in role deletion is blocked.

UC-007.Name: PresetSwitching
UC-007.Action: User lists, shows, or applies a named vvoc preset.
UC-007.Goal: Switch model-role assignments through declarative presets without rewriting OpenCode model fields directly.
UC-007.Acceptance: Preset application updates only roles declared by the selected preset, leaves unlisted roles unchanged, preserves user-owned presets, and keeps OpenCode config role-reference based.

UC-008.Name: SpecToCodeSkills
UC-008.Action: User asks for feature design, implementation planning, plan execution, review-only findings, durable reflection, or an end-of-session handoff.
UC-008.Goal: Route larger or higher-risk work through managed skills such as vv-spec, vv-plan, vv-execute, vv-review, vv-reflect, and vv-handoff.
UC-008.Acceptance: Spec and plan artifacts are created before large implementation waves, review-only requests return findings before fixes, long sessions can preserve reusable lessons or runbooks when approved, and session handoffs can be written to .vvoc/handoff/YYYY-MM-DD-<session-slug>/handoff.xml from already-visible context.

UC-009.Name: WorkItemTracking
UC-009.Action: Controller opens, lists, or closes explicit workflow work items.
UC-009.Goal: Replace free-form multi-agent loops with explicit work-item identity, mode, required reviewers, lifecycle state, and review-round metadata.
UC-009.Acceptance: work_item_open requires stable keys, title, mode, and required reviewers; repeated matching opens are idempotent; list returns state; close succeeds only when the item is ready to close.

UC-010.Name: TrackedLaunchEnforcement
UC-010.Action: Main session launches vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, or vv-code-reviewer through the task tool.
UC-010.Goal: Ensure every tracked subagent launch belongs to a valid open work item and follows the stored workflow state.
UC-010.Acceptance: Missing, malformed, unknown, duplicate, invalid transition, after-hard-stop, and round-overflow launches are rejected; untracked task launches pass through unaffected.

UC-011.Name: TrackedResultParsing
UC-011.Action: A tracked subagent completes and returns protocol fields.
UC-011.Goal: Parse machine-checkable top-block results, validate work-item identity and status, update workflow state, collect reviewer results, and fail closed on malformed output.
UC-011.Acceptance: Valid results update state and counters deterministically, review-only FAIL is reported as completed findings, NEEDS_CONTEXT and BLOCKED halt automation, and malformed output triggers bounded repair only when safely resumable.

UC-012.Name: ReviewOnlyWorkflow
UC-012.Action: User asks for review findings rather than fixes.
UC-012.Goal: Run spec and/or code reviewers through review-only work items and stop after reporting findings.
UC-012.Acceptance: Required reviewers are collected independently, reviewer FAIL is a completed review result, and the workflow does not route to implementation unless the user explicitly asks for fixes.

UC-013.Name: LoopGateAndHardStops
UC-013.Action: A tracked implementation and review cycle repeats or a hard-stop status appears.
UC-013.Goal: Prevent review churn and unsafe continuation by using explicit counters and hard-stop states.
UC-013.Acceptance: Rounds one and two are allowed, round three is rejected with next-action guidance, NEEDS_CONTEXT and BLOCKED always return control to the main session or user, and no semantic similarity heuristics are used.

UC-014.Name: WorkflowAndSystemGuidance
UC-014.Action: An eligible primary OpenCode session starts while vv-opencode plugins are active.
UC-014.Goal: Inject reusable process guidance and workflow protocol instructions into primary sessions without polluting managed subagents.
UC-014.Acceptance: vv-controller and other eligible primaries receive route-selection, context-gathering, editing, repository-memory, and work-item guidance; helper primaries and known subagents are skipped.

UC-015.Name: GuardianSafety
UC-015.Action: OpenCode asks for a permission decision while Guardian is enabled.
UC-015.Goal: Keep routine long-running work moving while leaving risky actions to manual approval.
UC-015.Acceptance: Guardian loads selected runtime config, resolves its model role, reviews bounded action context, auto-approves only low-risk decisions, and falls back to manual approval on uncertainty or failure.

UC-016.Name: SecretsRedaction
UC-016.Action: Provider-bound messages, tool inputs, or tool outputs contain secrets while redaction is enabled.
UC-016.Goal: Reduce accidental leakage by replacing secrets before model requests and restoring placeholders where local execution needs original values.
UC-016.Acceptance: Configured and built-in secret patterns are redacted before provider-bound requests, restored after completions and before tool execution, and kept stable within bounded session state.

UC-017.Name: HashlineEditSafety
UC-017.Action: Agent reads and edits text files while HashlineEditPlugin is enabled.
UC-017.Goal: Make edits safer by tying file changes to fresh line and context anchors rather than stale line numbers.
UC-017.Acceptance: Read output provides usable anchors, valid anchored edits preserve file text envelope, stale or malformed edit references fail safely, and ambiguous line-only changes are not applied.

UC-018.Name: PluginToggles
UC-018.Action: User enables or disables vvoc-managed plugins through CLI or selected vvoc config.
UC-018.Goal: Allow users to intentionally disable or re-enable individual vvoc-managed plugins without uninstalling the package.
UC-018.Acceptance: Known plugin names round-trip in the selected config, sync preserves toggle values, disabled plugins return empty hooks on next session, and confirmation output tells the user to restart.

UC-019.Name: PluginVisibility
UC-019.Goal: Show both OpenCode-level plugin registration and vvoc-level enabled or disabled state for managed plugins.
UC-019.Acceptance: Plugin list displays registered plugin entries, source paths, and vvoc toggle status when selected vvoc config is valid; missing or invalid vvoc config does not crash OpenCode plugin listing.

UC-020.Name: UpgradeAndPartialRecovery
UC-020.Action: User runs vvoc upgrade and the follow-up sync cannot complete.
UC-020.Goal: Allow package upgrade to succeed while making config refresh failure explicit and recoverable.
UC-020.Acceptance: Already-latest, network failure, install failure, successful install, and post-install sync failure are reported distinctly; partial sync failure leaves config unchanged and tells the user how to recover.

NonGoals.NonGoal: Do not replace OpenCode itself; vv-opencode layers process, managed config, skills, and plugins on top of OpenCode.
Do not infer workflow identity through semantic similarity, embeddings, n-grams, or fuzzy matching.
Do not silently repair invalid user-owned configuration during mutating commands.
Do not track every possible agent in the strict workflow state machine; workflow tracking is limited to managed tracked subagents.
Do not manage enable or disable state for arbitrary user-defined OpenCode plugins; plugin toggles apply only to vvoc-managed plugins.
Do not publish packages automatically from normal branch pushes.
Do not edit generated dist output manually.

Constraints.Constraint: OpenCode config remains in OpenCode-managed paths; vvoc must not invent unrelated config locations.
vvoc-managed config may be global under $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/ or project-local under .vvoc/, depending on selected scope and command semantics.
Project-scope writes must stay inside project-local .opencode/ and .vvoc/ paths.
Persisted vvoc data must live under $XDG_DATA_HOME/vvoc/.
Config writes must be conservative, idempotent, and strict about current schema validity before mutation.
Role resolution must fail loudly for unknown or invalid semantic role references.
Workflow enforcement must use explicit work-item IDs and simple counters only.
NEEDS_CONTEXT and BLOCKED are hard stops that return control to the main session or user.
Tracked workflow subagents use vv-* names: vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, and vv-code-reviewer.
Project-owned specs and plans produced by vvoc skills live under date-prefixed package directories .vvoc/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>/.
Hashline-backed editing must fail safely on stale or malformed edit references instead of applying ambiguous line-based changes.
Package exports are public API and must remain valid unless an explicit breaking change is approved.

Risks.Risk: Shared workflows may reference custom roles not assigned on another machine, causing startup failures.
Unsafe rewrites of user-maintained config could damage trust in the CLI.
OpenCode plugin and SDK surface area may evolve.
Provider alias models may be required for some role assignments.
LLMs may produce malformed tracked subagent protocol output.
Workflow state can confuse users after process restarts, session switches, or corrupt persistence if diagnostics are weak.
Forcing default_agent to vv-controller may surprise users who intentionally selected another default.
Plugin toggles can disable critical safety or workflow behavior unless status and doctor output make that visible.
Strict schema behavior can frustrate users with hand-edited configs unless errors include precise recovery steps.

OpenQuestions.Question: Should future versions validate role assignments against provider-declared capabilities such as vision?
Should diagnostics discover literal provider/model strings still present in OpenCode config fields?
Should investigator become a tracked agent in a future workflow version?
Should work-item state expose an explicit user-facing recovery or import path after session loss or state corruption?
Should GRACE project context eventually reference vvoc spec packages directly, or should .vvoc/specs remain a separate workflow artifact surface?
